title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is concentrated in governance rather than new technical safety evidence: OpenAI published a cluster of policy documents spanning frontier oversight, youth protections, and its own advocacy posture. The most material update is a proposed U.S. framework for frontier AI safety and resilience, with supporting items on standards, safeguards, and political transparency.

items.title: OpenAI proposes federal blueprint for frontier AI governance
items.why_it_matters: It advances a concrete governance framework for frontier-model safety, resilience, and national-security oversight.

items.title: OpenAI sets out public policy agenda on safety, youth protection, and standards
items.why_it_matters: It signals the company’s priorities for regulation, safeguards, workforce transition, and international AI standards.

items.title: OpenAI calls for global action on youth AI safety
items.why_it_matters: It frames youth safety as a governance and standards issue, not just a product-design concern.

items.title: OpenAI outlines its approach to AI policy and political advocacy
items.why_it_matters: It clarifies how the company represents its policy positions and supports regulation and safety efforts.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
